tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Big 3/4oz spinnerbait with single willow blade for offshore structure — let it helicopter down on a tight line feeling the blade turn, rip it off the bottom, let it flutter back. Mimics fleeing shad. 7-foot rod for big jumps.'

KVD: 'Slow-roll spinnerbaits along tapering grass lines, standing timber, submerged structures. Reel just fast enough to skim cover. When it touches an obstacle, speed up and let it flutter back — that provokes the reaction strike.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Offshore spinnerbait: 3/4oz, trim wire down a third, single willow blade. Cast, let helicopter down to bottom on tight line. Rip up sharply, let flutter back down. Provokes reaction strikes from suspended bass in summer heat.'

KVD: 'Tandem willow blades for horizontal retrieves through grass — less lift, runs deeper. Single blade for vertical presentations and helicopter flutter. Colorado/willow combo for slower retrieves and longer time in strike zone.'

KVD: 'Position boat to fish parallel to edges and cover. Keeps the bait in the strike zone longer. Short accurate casts (30-40ft) for control in dense cover. Soft underhand presentations so bait lands quietly in shallow water.'
